SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper gives clear formalizations (dropout as an LVM), states the main claims, and supplies proofs for its theorems (e.g. Theorems 1–4 and proofs in the appendix) that appear mathematically consistent under the stated assumptions (Jensen, Taylor expansions, operator-norm bounds). Experimental methodology is reasonable (comparisons to standard dropout, MC dropout, and dropout distillation; validation-based hyperparameter tuning; mean±std over 5 runs), though no statistical significance tests are reported. Some results rely on restrictive or unverifiable assumptions (bounded operator norms, uniqueness of best prediction in Theorem 5, distributions being C-close), which should be highlighted as limiting conditions; these do not fatally undermine the main contributions but temper generality.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper presents a novel viewpoint by formulating dropout explicitly as a latent variable model and introducing the concept of (approximate) expectation-linearity to quantify the train/test inference gap, plus a practical regularizer derived from that notion. While it builds on prior analyses of dropout and related ideas (e.g. Monte Carlo dropout, dropout distillation, Bayesian interpretations), the particular formalization and the expectation-linearization regularizer are non-trivial and distinguishable from prior work. The idea is not a radical departure but a meaningful and useful new perspective and method.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (introduction, model, theory, algorithm, experiments, appendix) and most notation is consistently defined; major results are stated up front and proven in appendices. Some portions of the provided text show duplication and minor formatting/repetition issues and referenced figures/tables are not visible here, but the core arguments and definitions (e.g. expectation-linearity, closeness) are communicated clearly. A reader with background in learning theory and deep nets can follow the development without undue difficulty.
